name: k6
what_it_does: JavaScript-scripted load testing tool using a virtual user (VU) model with scenarios, thresholds, and Grafana Cloud integration for performance benchmarking.
use_cases: Write load test scripts in ES6 JavaScript to simulate user flows against HTTP APIs and measure latency, error rate, and throughput
Define scenarios with multiple executors (constant-vus, ramping-vus, constant-arrival-rate) to model ramp-up, sustained load, and spike patterns
Set thresholds (e.g., http_req_duration p(95)<500) that cause the test to fail automatically when performance SLOs are breached
Use k6 Cloud for distributed load generation from multiple geographic regions without managing infrastructure
Extend tests with xk6 extensions (e.g., xk6-browser for browser-based load, xk6-kafka for message queue testing)
not_for: Unit or functional correctness testing — k6 measures performance, not behavioral correctness
Full browser E2E test suites — xk6-browser exists but k6's strength is protocol-level HTTP load generation
Python or non-JavaScript scripting environments where Locust offers a more natural workflow
best_when: Benchmarking HTTP API performance under load with declarative thresholds and CI-friendly pass/fail outcomes.
avoid_when: You need to validate business logic correctness or need a Python-native load testing workflow.

auth.notes: Local tool — no auth needed. k6 Cloud uses API token authentication for distributed runs.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Unlimited local runs; k6 Cloud free tier has limited VUh per month
pricing.paid_tiers: k6 Cloud paid tiers for higher VUh, longer tests, and team features
pricing.notes: Open source (AGPL-3.0). k6 Cloud (Grafana Cloud k6) is separately priced.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: k6 outputs structured end-of-test summaries with metric values and threshold results; script errors include line numbers from the JS source.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Load tests are repeatable when the target system is in a consistent state; results will vary with system load and network conditions.
known_agent_gotchas: k6 runs scripts in a custom JS runtime (Goja), NOT Node.js — npm packages that use Node.js built-ins (fs, path, crypto) or CommonJS require() will fail; only ES6 modules and k6 built-ins are available

The default function must export a function, not an async function — k6 handles async via its own event loop; using async/await in the default function body requires the experimental async support flag

VU code runs in parallel across virtual users, but init code (top-level module scope) runs once per VU — agents must not place shared state mutations in init code expecting them to be visible across VUs
Thresholds are evaluated at the end of the test by default — a threshold breach does not stop the test mid-run unless abortOnFail: true is set in the threshold config
check() in k6 does not throw on failure and does not stop test execution — it records a pass/fail metric; agents must combine checks with thresholds to make test failures visible in CI output

security_score_components.security_notes: Auth tokens for load-tested services should be passed via __ENV variables; k6 scripts committed to version control must not contain hardcoded credentials.
